mnemonics.title: ZOMBIE Process Memory
mnemonics.content: ZOMBIE: Z - Zero (exit status stored), O - Orphaned but tracked, M - Memory freed but PCB remains, B - Blocked from complete removal, I - Incomplete cleanup, E - Entry in table stays

mnemonics.title: Exit vs _exit
mnemonics.content: exit = 'eXit completely' = flush buffers + call handlers + _exit; _exit = '_' (underscore) = just exit = immediate termination

mnemonics.title: Signal Priority
mnemonics.content: KILL > STOP > TERM: K - Kill (can't ignore), S - Stop (can catch), T - Term (graceful)

acronyms.usage: Data structure containing process information, removed after wait() by parent
Signal sent to parent when child terminates; parent should handle this to call wait()
Option for waitpid() to return immediately if no child has terminated
Macro to check if child exited normally via exit() call

visualTips.visualization: Draw a process table as a grid. Show child in 'ZOMBIE' box with dotted outline, still occupying one row, while memory block is empty/greyed out
Show parent-child tree: when parent dies, draw arrow from children to 'init' box (PID 1), showing adoption process
Two flowcharts: exit() shows 'flush buffers → call handlers → _exit()', while _exit() shows direct arrow to 'terminate'

analogies.analogy: Like a person who has died but the death certificate hasn't been filed - they exist on paper but are functionally gone
Like children whose parents died - they get taken to an orphanage (init process) where someone cares for them
Like collecting a finished job's report - the worker (child) is done, but the manager (parent) must collect the results to close the file
